title: Savory Smoked Irish Bacon Gravy-over-Fried-Hash
description: A tangy, crispy Irish breakfast dish featuring smoked Irish bacon, ginger garlic paste, and a fodmap-friendly twist.

ingredients.name: Smoked Irish bacon
ingredients.notes: Look for a high-quality, low-sodium brand

ingredients.name: All-purpose gluten-free flour
ingredients.notes: Use a 1:1 substitute for traditional flour

instructions: In a large bowl, combine the grated potato and egg, mixing until the potato is evenly coated.
Transfer the potato mixture to a plate or tray and place it in the freezer to chill for 30 minutes.
While the potato mixture is chilling, combine the smoked Irish bacon, onion, and a sprinkle of salt and pepper in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Cook the bacon mixture, stirring occasionally, until the bacon is crispy and golden brown, about 10 minutes.
Remove the skillet from the heat and set it aside to cool slightly.
Once the potato mixture has frozen, remove it from the freezer and add it to the skillet with the bacon mixture.
Stir to combine the potato and bacon mixture, then return the skillet to medium-high heat.
Cook the mixture, stirring constantly, until the potatoes are crispy and golden brown, about 5 minutes.
While the potatoes are cooking, combine the ginger, garlic, and all-purpose gluten-free flour in a small bowl to make a paste.
Add the ginger garlic paste to the skillet with the potatoes and cook, stirring constantly, for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and stir in the salt and black pepper.
Serve the crispy potato and bacon mixture in a shallow dish, topped with a rich gravy made from the pan drippings and a bit more flour to thicken.
Use a whisk or fork to break up any lumps in the gravy, then serve it hot over the top of the crispy potatoes and bacon.
